operating_ranges.evidence: MAX THICKNESS OF FRAME SUPPORT IS 1/4" TO CLEAR TRI-CLAMP HANDLE
The ceramic fiber felt should extend at least 25 mm (0.98 in) beyond the front of the module

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Supporting the module by clamping the module body with U-bolts to system framing may severely, and irreversibly, damage the module – cause it to crack
must be designed to allow for the module's thermal expansion during sanitization
To account for furnace, module tolerances, and module shrinkage, and to prevent radiant losses through module gaps

fault_conditions.name: excessive_connection_stress
fault_conditions.description: Damage to module connections from improper support
fault_conditions.evidence: stress on the module's connections would be excessive

fault_conditions.name: thermal_expansion_constraint
fault_conditions.description: Restriction of thermal expansion during operation
fault_conditions.evidence: AVOID CLAMPING THE MODULE BODY TO FRAMING TO ALLOW FOR ITS THERMAL EXPANSIONS

maintenance_actions.name: ceramic_felt_replacement
maintenance_actions.evidence: placing a double-folded layer of ceramic fiber felt between Fibrothal® modules

maintenance_actions.name: mounting_inspection
maintenance_actions.evidence: modules not be supported by the piping alone, as the stress on the module's connections would be excessive

assumptions.statement: Mounting hardware includes ceramic cups, threaded connections, and fiber plugs based on component descriptions
assumptions.reason: Manual describes mounting components including ceramic cups, nuts, washers, and fixing pins as part of module mounting system

assumptions.statement: Thermal expansion accommodation is critical for mounting design
assumptions.reason: Multiple references to thermal expansion requirements and warnings about constraining module movement
